data.body:
=== RESEARCH REPORT ===
=== DSO re-explore: gate/hook ENFORCEMENT layer (fail-open/closed, bypass envelope)
=== agent aceebf0a1495433ca

I have everything needed. `get_artifacts_dir` is keyed off `/tmp/workflow-plugin-<hash(repo_root)>` — so the marker is **repo-scoped, not session-scoped** (the "session" framing in the marker comments is loose: any prior review in this repo's `/tmp` dir satisfies it; it persists across Claude sessions). This is a meaningful divergence point. I now have all the mechanics.

---

# DSO Hook / Gate Enforcement Layer — Mechanics

Root: `~/digital-service-orchestra/plugins/dso/`. Findings below are per-gate, with exact `file:line`. The most load-bearing distinction throughout is **fail-open vs fail-closed**, which splits cleanly along an explicit **gate-tier doctrine** (header tag `# DSO-GATE-TIER: A` = safety-critical/fail-closed; the soft/advisory hooks carry an ERR trap that forces `exit 0`).

## The two enforcement "boundaries"

DSO marks enforcement-class hooks with `# hook-boundary: enforcement` + `# DSO-GATE-TIER: A` in their header. Three files carry that tag: `review-gate.sh`, `pre-commit-review-gate.sh`, `review-integrity-guard.sh`. Everything else (plan-review-gate, brainstorm-gate, review-stop-check) is soft/advisory and fails open by construction.

A repo-wide kill switch sits above all enforcement hooks: `enforcement-gate.sh` (`_dso_enforcement_gate_check`) reads `dso.workflow` from config; when `=ci-pr` it short-circuits the hook to `exit 0` (enforcement is delegated to CI/PR instead of local hooks). Default/absent is `local` (hooks run). `enforcement-gate.sh:38-54`.

---

## GATE 1 — plan-review-gate (the direct precedent for your open→in_progress gate)

Two equivalent code paths exist (files-install wrapper + plugin dispatcher path); they are byte-for-byte the same logic.

- **Wrapper entry point:** `hooks/plan-review-gate.sh` (the file host projects wire directly via settings.json).
- **Live plugin path:** `plugin.json` PreToolUse ExitPlanMode matcher → `dispatchers/pre-exitplanmode.sh` → `hook_plan_review_gate` in `lib/session-misc-functions.sh:608-655`.

| Aspect | Mechanics |
|---|---|
| Trigger | PreToolUse on tool `ExitPlanMode`. Non-ExitPlanMode → `exit 0` immediately (`plan-review-gate.sh:31-33`; `session-misc-functions.sh:615-617`). |
| What it checks | Existence + first line of marker file `$ARTIFACTS_DIR/plan-review-status` (`plan-review-gate.sh:42`). Block if file missing (`:45-56`); block if first line `!= "passed"` (`:59-68`). That's the entire check — no schema, no findings, no hash. |
| Block signal | `exit 2` (wrapper) / `return 2` (function). |
| Fail-open vs closed | **FAILS OPEN.** Two independent fail-open paths: (1) not in a git repo → `exit 0` (`plan-review-gate.sh:36-39`); (2) any internal error trips the ERR trap which logs to `dso-hook-errors.jsonl` and forces `exit 0` (`plan-review-gate.sh:21`; function-level trap `session-misc-functions.sh:611`). |
| Bypass mechanism | **No force flag, no env var, no sentinel.** The only way past is to make the marker say `passed` — i.e., run `/dso:plan-review`. (Direct `echo passed > .../plan-review-status` is *not* blocked by review-integrity-guard — that guard explicitly exempts `plan-review-status`; see Gate 5.) |
| What writes the artifact | The `plan-review` skill, `skills/plan-review/SKILL.md` Step 3. It writes `passed\ntimestamp=…\nartifact_type=…\nverdict={PASS\|REVISE}\nscores=…`. **Critical nuance:** the first line is hardcoded `passed` and the marker is written on **both** PASS and REVISE-then-fixed outcomes (SKILL.md Step 2 items 1 & 2 both "Write the marker file"). So the gate's `first-line==passed` check passes even when the verdict was REVISE — the gate only proves *a review ran*, not that it cleared. |
| Type/hierarchy branching | None in the gate. Branching is upstream in the skill: `artifact_type: design→opus`, `implementation_plan→sonnet` (SKILL.md:36-38). |

**Marker scoping (divergence-relevant):** `$ARTIFACTS_DIR` = `get_artifacts_dir()` = `/tmp/workflow-plugin-<sha(repo_root):16>` (`deps.sh:265`+). It is **repo-scoped, not session-scoped** — despite the "for this session" wording in the hook's own comments, the marker lives in `/tmp` keyed only by repo path and survives across Claude sessions until `/tmp` is cleared. A stale `passed` marker from any earlier session in the same repo satisfies the gate.

---

## GATE 2 — pre-commit-review-gate (Layer 1, the heavyweight fail-closed gate)

`hooks/pre-commit-review-gate.sh`, registered as a git `pre-commit` local hook (NOT via `core.hooksPath` — header `:26-28`). Tagged `DSO-GATE-TIER: A`. This is the real precedent for a fail-closed, hash-bound, artifact-verifying gate.

- **Trigger:** git pre-commit (every `git commit`). Reads staged files via `git diff --cached --name-only` (`:243`).
- **Enforcement kill switch first:** `_dso_enforcement_gate_check && exit 0` (`:49`) — ci-pr mode disables it.
- **Allowlist short-circuit:** loads `lib/review-gate-allowlist.conf`; if *all* staged files match the allowlist regex → `exit 0` no review needed (`:354-357`). If the allowlist fails to load, `NON_REVIEWABLE_REGEX` is empty → **everything requires review (fail-safe)** (`:341-343`).
- **What it checks for non-allowlisted files** (`:441-530`):
  1. `$ARTIFACTS_DIR/review-status` exists, else `exit 1` "No review recorded" (`:448-452`).
  2. First line `== "passed"`, else `exit 1` (`:457-462`). (Note: here `passed` is severity-derived by `record-review.sh`, so unlike the plan gate it *does* mean the review cleared — see Gate 4.)
  3. `diff_hash=` line present (`:465-470`) and **equals the freshly recomputed `compute-diff-hash.sh` of the current staged diff** (`:474`, `:495`). This binds the recorded review to the exact code state. Mismatch → `exit 1` (`:521-523`).
- **Block signal:** `exit 1` for the normal block paths; `exit 2` specifically on the hash-compute-failure path (see below).
- **Fail-open vs closed:** **FAILS CLOSED throughout**, and one path was explicitly reclassified from open→closed:
  - Hash computation returning empty used to fail open. Now (`:475-493`) it emits `GATE_UNAVAILABLE` via `_dso_gate_unavailable review_gate "hash_compute_failed"` and `exit 2` (block) **unless** the paired bypass env vars are set.
  - Missing arbiter helper → fail closed (`:225-229`).
  - Malformed arbiter sidecar JSON → fail closed (`_check_arbiter_sidecar_and_block`, `:218-235`).
  - No allowlist → fail safe (review everything).
- **Bypass mechanisms (several, all auditable):**
  - `DSO_GATE_BYPASS_REVIEW_GATE=1` + `DSO_GATE_BYPASS_REVIEW_GATE_REASON='<reason>'` — the paired-env-var envelope, only honored on the hash-compute-failure path (`:482-486`).
  - `DSO_MECHANICAL_AMEND=1` → `exit 0` (`:264-266`), for merge-to-main.sh's mechanical amends. (Layer 2 sentinel blocks this on non-amend commits — Gate 3 Pattern j.)
  - WIP — handled by Layer 2 (sentinel), not here.
  - Merge/rebase in progress → incoming-only files filtered out; if all staged are incoming-only → `exit 0` (`:280-312`).
- **Self-heal (drift forgiveness):** on hash mismatch it checks `_is_formatting_only_drift` (ruff-format-only `.py` changes, `:165-211`) or `_is_shellcheck_disable_only_drift` (shellcheck-directive-only `.sh` changes, `:129-146`); if true, it *rewrites* the recorded `diff_hash` and allows the commit (`:506-520`). Requires `ruff` in PATH or it returns "can't verify" → no heal → block.
- **Extra fail-closed sub-gates:** reviewer-agent staleness (content-hash of generated agent vs source fragments, `:366-439`, `exit 1` if stale); overlay-missing gate (`:712-737` in record-review actually; the pre-commit one is the staleness + arbiter sidecar). 
- **Type/hierarchy branching:** none on ticket type; branching is on file class (allowlisted vs not, `.py`/`.sh` for self-heal).

---

## GATE 3 — review-gate / review-bypass-sentinel (Layer 2, blocks the escape hatches)

`hooks/review-gate.sh` is a 6-line wrapper → `hook_review_bypass_sentinel` in `lib/review-gate-bypass-sentinel.sh`. Plugin path: `dispatchers/pre-bash.sh`. Tagged `DSO-GATE-TIER: A`.

- **Trigger:** PreToolUse on tool `Bash` only (`:42-45`). Enforcement kill switch first (`:35`).
- **What it checks:** pattern-matches the command string for bypass vectors. Patterns (`:108-280`): (a) `--no-verify`; (b) `-n` short-flag in a git-commit context; (c) `core.hooksPath=`; (d) `git commit-tree`; (e) `git update-ref` (exempt if invoked by merge-to-main.sh); (f) writes to `.git/hooks/`; (g) writes to `test-gate-status`/`test-status/`; (h) deletes of same; (i) writes to `test-exemptions`; (j) `DSO_MECHANICAL_AMEND=1` on a non-amend commit; (k) direct writes/deletes into `.tickets-tracker/`.
- **Block signal:** `return 2`.
- **Fail-open vs closed:** **Mostly fail-closed on the parse path** (notable, and directly relevant to you): if the command can't be extracted by the bash parser it does NOT wave through — it retries with a `python3 json.load` pass, and if still empty it `return 2` blocks an "unverifiable command" (`:50-76`). Quote `:66-75`: *"Genuinely unparsable… Fail closed rather than wave a potential bypass through unchecked."* The ERR trap does force `return 0` on internal error (`:38`), so a *crash* fails open, but an *empty/garbled command* fails closed.
- **Bypass mechanisms (legitimate):**
  - **WIP exemption** — command containing standalone word `WIP` (case-insensitive, word-boundary) → `return 0` (`:78-82`).
  - Quoted-string stripping before matching (`COMMAND_STRIPPED`, `:91-106`) so ticket descriptions mentioning "--no-verify" don't false-positive.
  - Per-pattern authorized-writer exemptions: `record-test-status.sh`, `record-test-exemption.sh`, ticket CLI scripts (`ticket-*.sh/.py`), `git -C .tickets-tracker`, merge-to-main.sh for update-ref.

---

## GATE 4 — record-review.sh (the artifact WRITER that the gates verify)

`hooks/record-review.sh`. Not a hook — the trusted writer of `$ARTIFACTS_DIR/review-status`. This is the integrity root: gates only ever *read* the file it writes.

- **Trigger:** invoked manually after `/dso:review` (or via `/dso:commit`). `set -euo pipefail` (`:40`) — **fails closed by default** (any error aborts before writing).
- **What it does / writes:** reads `reviewer-findings.json` (written by the sub-agent via write-reviewer-findings.sh — Gate 6), verifies its SHA-256 against `--reviewer-hash` (or the `.sha256` sidecar, `:241-280`) — **file-tamper detection**. Then a Python validator (`:291-338`) **derives** status from `findings[].severity`: `critical|important|fragile ⇒ failed`, else `passed` (`:318-319`). The description text cannot change status — only severity. Writes `review-status` (`:752-758`):
  ```
  <passed|failed>
  timestamp=…
  diff_hash=<compute-diff-hash.sh>
  review_hash=<sha256 of findings>
  ```
- **Anti-fabrication invariants:**
  - No stdin JSON accepted — only the hash-verified findings file (header `:6-9`). This is *why* the review-integrity-guard (Gate 5) blocks direct writes.
  - 64-hex SHA validation of the reviewer hash (`:266-271`).
  - Files-overlap check: finding `file` fields must overlap actually-changed files (`:518-550`), and per-finding strip removes out-of-diff (hallucinated) findings then re-derives status (`:408-510`).
  - `--expected-hash` rejects recording against a diff that changed since dispatch (`:560-611`).
- **Tier enforcement (fail-OPEN):** verifies `review_tier >= selected_tier` (light=1/standard=2/deep=3), taking max of findings-self-reported and classifier-telemetry to stop self-downgrade (`:616-697`). When neither tier source is present → **fail-open**, allow, and stamp `tier_verified=false` into the file (`:684-689`, `:760-763`). A downgrade detected → `exit 1`.
- **Overlay enforcement (fail-CLOSED):** if classifier telemetry flagged overlays for this diff but the helper `read-overlay-flags.sh` is missing → `exit 1` (`:717-724`); flagged overlay with no `reviewer-findings-<dim>.json` → `exit 1` (`:733-736`).
- **`--attest` mode:** transfers a `passed` review-status from a worktree artifacts dir into the session, re-stamping the current diff hash (`:150-209`) — the cross-worktree provenance bridge.

---

## GATE 5 — review-integrity-guard (protects the artifact from forgery)

`hooks/review-integrity-guard.sh` (5-line wrapper) → `hook_review_integrity_guard` in `lib/pre-bash-functions.sh:614-642`. Tagged `DSO-GATE-TIER: A`.

- **Trigger:** PreToolUse Bash. Extracts `.tool_input.command`; empty command → `return 0` (`:621-623`).
- **What it checks:** regex `(>|>>|tee)\s*\S*review-status` — i.e. any shell redirect/tee targeting a `review-status` file (`:631`).
- **Block signal:** `return 2`.
- **Fail-open vs closed:** ERR trap forces `return 0` on internal error (`:617`) — crash fails open; empty command fails open (`:621`). The *match* itself is fail-closed (blocks).
- **Bypass / exemptions (two):** command containing `record-review.sh` → allowed (the one legitimate writer, `:626-628`); target containing `plan-review-status` → allowed (`:632-634`). **This is why the plan-review marker (Gate 1) is forgeable by a direct echo while the code-review marker is not** — the guard explicitly carves out `plan-review-status`.

---

## GATE 6 — write-reviewer-findings.sh (validate-then-write for the findings file)

`hooks/write-reviewer-findings.sh` is a shim → canonical `scripts/write-reviewer-findings.sh`.

- **Mechanic:** writes piped JSON to a *pending* file, runs schema validation (`validate-review-output.sh code-review-dispatch`), and only on pass `mv`s it to canonical `reviewer-findings.json`, emits the `.sha256` sidecar, and prints the hash (`:153-168`). Exit 1 on schema failure (nothing written), exit 2 on usage error. Header `:5-9`: *"Only outputs the SHA-256 hash on success — making it mechanically impossible to obtain a valid REVIEWER_HASH without passing schema validation."* This is the cryptographic chokepoint: no valid hash ⇒ record-review.sh can't accept it ⇒ no `passed` review-status.

---

## GATE 7 — review-stop-check (SOFT warning, never blocks)

`hooks/review-stop-check.sh` (+ identical `hook_review_stop_check`, `session-misc-functions.sh:224-301`).

- **Trigger:** Stop hook (Claude finishes responding).
- **What it checks:** uncommitted/staged/untracked changes exist AND (no review-status OR status≠passed OR `diff_hash` stale) → prints a `# REMINDER`.
- **Block signal:** **none** — always `exit 0`. Header `:9` explicitly: *"This is a SOFT GATE (warning). It does not block."* ERR trap forces exit 0 (`:21`).
- **Relevance:** the model for an advisory (AMBIGUOUS→surface-but-overridable) tier in your design — same artifact (`review-status`), same hash-staleness check, zero enforcement.

---

## GATE 8 — brainstorm-gate (parallel to plan-review-gate, with an allowlist bypass)

`dispatchers/pre-enterplanmode.sh` → `hook_brainstorm_gate` (`session-misc-functions.sh:670-754`). No separate wrapper file; dispatcher-only.

- **Trigger:** PreToolUse on `EnterPlanMode`. Non-match / non-repo → return 0.
- **Config switch:** `brainstorm.enforce_entry_gate` (default true); `false` → return 0 (`:690-694`).
- **What it checks:** sentinel `$ARTIFACTS_DIR/brainstorm-sentinel` exists (`:738-751`).
- **Block:** `return 2`; **fail-open** (ERR trap → return 0, `:673`).
- **Bypass:** a hardcoded **allowlist** of skills (`fix-bug, debug-everything, sprint, implementation-plan, preplanning, resolve-conflicts, architect-foundation, retro`, `:713-723`) read from `$ARTIFACTS_DIR/active-skill-context` — if the active skill is allowlisted, gate is bypassed (`:725-736`). This is a different bypass model from the env-var envelope: **artifact-declares-context** rather than operator-overrides.

---

## bypass-sentinel design (how a legitimate override is recorded) — `lib/gate-unavailable.sh`

This is the canonical, auditable override envelope used by Tier-A gates. Two functions:

- `_dso_gate_bypass_active <gate_name>` (`:133-193`): returns 0 (bypass authorized) **only if BOTH** `DSO_GATE_BYPASS_<UPPER_NAME>=1` **and** `DSO_GATE_BYPASS_<UPPER_NAME>_REASON=<non-empty>` are set. Flag-without-reason is explicitly rejected with `BYPASS_REJECTED` (`:161-165`) — **you cannot bypass anonymously; a justification string is mandatory.** Gate names are validated against `[a-z_][a-z0-9_]*` so they form valid env-var suffixes (`:89-92`, `:141-145`). When active it appends a JSONL audit record (`{event:"bypass_active", gate_name, reason, session_id, ticket_id, actor}`) to `~/.claude/logs/dso-gate-unavailable.jsonl` and echoes `BYPASS_ACTIVE` to stderr (`:167-192`).
- `_dso_gate_unavailable <gate_name> <reason>` (`:95-130`): records a `GATE_UNAVAILABLE` JSONL audit record (same fields, no `event` key) + stderr mirror. Best-effort write, never blocks on log failure.

The contract (header `:21-22`): **Tier A callers `return 2` (block) after `_dso_gate_unavailable` unless `_dso_gate_bypass_active` returns 0; Tier C callers just record and proceed.** session_id/ticket_id resolved from a fallback chain of env vars, defaulting to `"unknown"` (`:42-64`). This is the design to mirror for your gate's "legitimate override" path: paired env vars (flag + reason), name-validated, audit-logged, fail-closed-by-default.

## gate-unavailable handling (what happens when the gate can't run)

Governed by the **gate-tier doctrine**: a gate that cannot run does NOT silently pass if it's Tier A.
- **Tier A (safety-critical):** infrastructure failure → emit `GATE_UNAVAILABLE` + **block (`return 2`)** unless the operator opted in via the paired bypass envelope. Live example: pre-commit-review-gate hash-compute failure (`pre-commit-review-gate.sh:475-493`) — explicitly reclassified from the old fail-open behavior.
- **Tier C (advisory):** record the event, proceed with normal degradation.
- The **soft hooks** (plan-review-gate, brainstorm-gate, review-stop-check) predate/sit outside this and **fail open** via ERR trap. So DSO has a deliberate split: the *enforcement* layer (commit-time) is fail-closed-on-unavailable; the *advisory* layer (plan-mode / stop) is fail-open.

## preconditions-validator (what preconditions block work) — `lib/preconditions-validator-lib.sh`

This is the closest thing to a *stage-boundary entry gate* (i.e., "can this stage start?"), which maps directly onto open→in_progress.

- **Source-only library** (not a hook), sourced by skill/workflow orchestrators (`:6-7`). Idempotent guard (`:25-26`).
- `_dso_pv_entry_check(stage, upstream_stage, ticket_id)` (`:163-192`): the **entry gate**. Reads the latest upstream `*-PRECONDITIONS.json` event for the ticket (session-agnostic, scans `.tickets-tracker/<ticket_id>/`, lexicographically latest by filename, `:101-155`) and validates **minimal-tier required fields**: `event_type`(must==`PRECONDITIONS`), `gate_name`, `session_id`, `worktree_id`, `tier`, `timestamp`, `data` (`:39-47`, `:52-91`). Missing upstream event → diagnostic + `return 1`. Schema-invalid → `return 1`. So the precondition that blocks downstream work is: **a valid, minimal-schema PRECONDITIONS event from the upstream stage must exist and be readable from the ticket's event dir.**
- `_dso_pv_exit_write(stage, upstream_event_id, spec_hash, ticket_id)` (`:199-247`): writes the stage's own PRECONDITIONS event (chaining `upstream_event_id` for cross-stage lineage, `:340-342`), then **roundtrip self-checks** the written file against the same schema; roundtrip failure → `return 1`.
- **Fail-open posture:** header `:20` and `:153` — *"Fail-open callers: both functions return non-zero on error; callers apply `|| true`."* The lib returns non-zero on missing/invalid, but the documented calling convention is `|| true`, so callers degrade rather than hard-block. This is the **opposite** posture from the commit gate. Depth-agnostic: unknown extra fields are silently accepted (`:88`) — forward-compat by design.
- **Config floor:** `_dso_pv_validate_inference_review_config` (`:253-280`) enforces `preconditions.inference_review.rate_high_stakes >= 100` (high-stakes decisions must always be challenged), `exit 1` below floor. (Run directly as a script, `:362-368`.)

## validate-review-schemas (the schema the plan-review output must satisfy) — `lib/validate-review-schemas.sh`

Two Python validators (sourced by `validate-review-output.sh`):
- `validate_review_protocol_base` (`:14-123`): strict JSON schema for the multi-perspective review protocol — top-level keys exactly `{subject, reviews, conflicts}` (extra keys rejected, `:33-36`); per-review `status∈{reviewed,not_applicable}`, dimension scores int 1-5-or-null, finding severities `∈{critical,major,minor}`; conflict `pattern∈{add_vs_remove,more_vs_less,strict_vs_flexible,expand_vs_reduce}`. `exit 1` on any violation.
- `validate_plan_review` (`:126-184`): validates the **plan-review structured-text** format — requires a `VERDICT:` line `∈{PASS,REVISE}` (`:140-146`); a `SCORES:` block with all four dims present (`feasibility, completeness, yagni, codebase_alignment`, 1-5, `:148-163`); `FINDINGS:` marker with `FINDING: [dim] [severity:…]` blocks validated against the same dim set + `{critical,major,minor}` (`:165-174`). This is the pre-gate the plan-review skill runs before writing the marker — but note the **gate hook (Gate 1) never re-checks this**; it only reads the marker's first line. The schema is enforced only at write-time by the skill, not at block-time by the hook.

---

## Reconciliation against the catalog (Part 4.4, `dso-dimension-catalog.md:170-174`)

Part 4.4 is **accurate but thin and slightly imprecise**. Confirmations and corrections:

**Confirmed:**
- PASS = all 4 ≥4; REVISE = any <4 (catalog :171) — confirmed (SKILL.md Step 2; schema `validate_plan_review`).
- artifact_type branch design→opus / implementation_plan→sonnet (:172) — confirmed (SKILL.md:36-38).
- marker file `plan-review-status`; schema-validation pre-gate; one-pass severity-ordered remediation (:173) — confirmed.
- plan-review-gate.sh: PreToolUse on ExitPlanMode; checks marker exists + first line `passed`; `exit 2` = block; **fails open** on non-repo / internal error (ERR trap); **no force flag**; bypass = run the review skill (:174) — **all confirmed exactly.**

**Corrections / precision to add:**
1. **"first line == passed" is weaker than the catalog implies.** The marker's first line is hardcoded `passed` by the skill on **both** PASS and REVISE-after-fix outcomes. So the plan gate proves *a review occurred*, not that it *cleared*. (Contrast Gate 2/4 where `passed` is severity-derived and genuinely means cleared.) This is a real semantic gap to design around.
2. **Marker is repo-scoped, not session-scoped.** Catalog and hook comments both say "for this session," but `get_artifacts_dir` keys off `/tmp/workflow-plugin-<sha(repo_root)>` (`deps.sh:265+`) with no session id. A stale marker from a prior session in the same repo passes the gate. Your "fire on claim" design must not inherit this — bind the marker to the ticket id + the ticket's current event state, not a `/tmp` repo hash.
3. **Two code paths, not one.** The catalog names only `plan-review-gate.sh`. The *live* plugin path is the dispatcher (`dispatchers/pre-exitplanmode.sh` → `hook_plan_review_gate` in `session-misc-functions.sh:608-655`); the `.sh` wrapper is the files-install entry. Both must stay in sync.
4. **The plan marker is forgeable; the code-review marker is not.** review-integrity-guard (Gate 5) explicitly exempts `plan-review-status` (`pre-bash-functions.sh:632-634`), so `echo passed > plan-review-status` bypasses Gate 1 entirely. The code-review equivalent is protected (guard + hash + validate-then-write chain). If your gate needs to be un-forgeable, copy the code-review artifact model (Gates 4/5/6), **not** the plan-review model.

**New material absent from the catalog entirely (the parts that most inform your gate):**
- The **gate-tier doctrine** (`DSO-GATE-TIER: A`, `hook-boundary: enforcement`) and the **fail-open (advisory) vs fail-closed (enforcement) split**. The catalog's synthesis note #3 says "plan-review-gate.sh fails OPEN; we fail CLOSED" — correct, but DSO *already has* a fail-closed precedent (`pre-commit-review-gate.sh`) the catalog never mentions. Mirror that, not the plan gate.
- The **paired-env-var bypass envelope** (`gate-unavailable.sh`): `DSO_GATE_BYPASS_<NAME>=1` + `_REASON='…'` (reason mandatory, name-validated, JSONL-audited). This is the override design to adopt for "legitimate override," and it is strictly better than the plan gate's no-bypass model.
- **gate-unavailable handling:** Tier A blocks-on-unavailable (the hash-compute path was explicitly reclassified open→closed) — directly answers "what happens when the gate can't run."
- The **hash-binding** mechanic (`diff_hash` recorded then re-verified against current state) — the analogue for binding an open→in_progress decision to the ticket's then-current event/state so a later edit invalidates it.
- The **preconditions-validator** stage-entry model (upstream PRECONDITIONS event must exist + pass minimal schema) — the closest conceptual match to a "can this stage start?" gate, though it is fail-open-by-convention (`|| true`).
- The full **write→guard→verify chain** for code-review artifacts (Gates 4/5/6): validate-then-write (only success yields a hash) → integrity-guard blocks direct writes → record-review verifies the hash + derives status from severity + binds the diff hash. That four-link chain is the anti-fabrication template.

**Files (all absolute):**
- `/Users/joeoakhart/digital-service-orchestra/plugins/dso/hooks/plan-review-gate.sh`
- `/Users/joeoakhart/digital-service-orchestra/plugins/dso/hooks/review-gate.sh`
- `/Users/joeoakhart/digital-service-orchestra/plugins/dso/hooks/pre-commit-review-gate.sh`
